title: On-Chain Attestation
description: Create cryptographic proofs of your data on Solana or Base blockchain. The data is hashed with SHA-256 and the hash is written on-chain as an immutable, timestamped record.

chain.title: Blockchain
chain.description:
Which blockchain to write the attestation to.

**Solana**: Fastest (~1 second), cheapest (~$0.001)
**Base**: Better for compliance use cases (~$0.02, 2-5 seconds)

data.title: Data to Attest
data.description: The JSON data you want to create a proof for. Only the SHA-256 hash goes on-chain (not the full data). You can attest any JSON structure.

description.title: Description
description.description: A human-readable description of what this attestation is for (e.g., 'Product pricing data from Amazon')
description.sectionDescription: Add context about your attestation. This metadata is included in the on-chain record.

source_url.title: Source URL
source_url.description: The URL where this data was scraped from (e.g., 'https://amazon.com/product/xyz')

actor_id.title: Actor ID
actor_id.description: The Apify Actor that produced this data (e.g., 'apify/amazon-scraper')

run_id.title: Run ID
run_id.description: The specific Actor run ID that produced this data

custom_metadata.title: Custom Metadata
custom_metadata.description: Any additional custom fields you want to include in the attestation record

store_full_data.title: Store full data in Apify
store_full_data.description: Save the complete data in Apify's Key-Value store alongside the on-chain hash. Useful for later verification.
store_full_data.sectionDescription: Configure how the attestation is stored and processed.

verify_mode.title: Verification mode
verify_mode.description: Enable this to verify an existing attestation instead of creating a new one. You'll need to provide the transaction hash below.
verify_mode.sectionDescription: Use this section to verify an existing attestation by comparing the on-chain hash with your data.

tx_hash.title: Transaction Hash to Verify
tx_hash.description: The blockchain transaction hash of the attestation you want to verify. Required when verification mode is enabled.
